Turn any pet photo into a stunning AI art portrait in 24 hours for $9—no artist, no wait, no expensive commissions.
Add tiered pricing radio group with Stripe Price ID mapping

Tech stack locked to existing: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Vercel Blob, Vercel Postgres. n8n for async workflow orchestration (self-hosted or cloud). Manus for AI portrait generation (internal Meta API, requires employee access). OpenAI GPT-4o for quality scoring (Vision API). Resend for transactional email. Stripe webhooks for order events. Quality threshold: 7/10 auto-approves, <7 queues for human review. Refund flow integrated via Stripe Refunds API. Admin auth: simple password-based middleware (upgrade to NextAuth if multiple admins needed). Database: Vercel Postgres with simple schema (quality_scores table tracks all generated portraits). No Redis/queue needed initially — n8n handles async processing, quality checks run synchronously on webhook receipt.
Creating design specs

Using Resend for email delivery (simpler Next.js integration than SendGrid), Stripe metadata + Customer balance for referral tracking (avoids database overhead), NextAuth with EmailProvider for passwordless auth (secure, low friction), Stripe Customer Portal for payment management (offloads PCI compliance), cookie-based referral attribution with 30-day expiry, React Email for type-safe templates, Vercel Edge Config or scheduled sends for email drip sequence.

Using Next.js built-in Script component for analytics (no extra deps). Embla Carousel chosen for testimonials (modern, TypeScript-friendly, tree-shakeable vs react-slick's jQuery dependency). Mailchimp API direct integration vs form action (allows custom discount code response). Exit-intent via mouseleave instead of dedicated library (reduces bundle size). localStorage for modal suppression (simple, works without backend). All components use 'use client' directive for interactivity. Environment variables follow Next.js NEXT_PUBLIC_ convention for client-side access.

Using React Server Components for success page to securely fetch Stripe session data server-side (keeps API keys safe). Client Components only for interactive modal (useEffect timer, state management). No additional dependencies needed beyond existing stripe SDK. Stripe Price IDs stored in .env.local for easy tier price updates without code changes. Metadata linking approach for upsells allows post-purchase analytics and refund handling. Table component uses pure Tailwind (no shadcn/ui) to match existing stack. Mobile-first responsive with CSS overflow-x-auto instead of JavaScript scroll libraries.

Using native React 18 Suspense (no external loading libraries) for skeleton states to minimize bundle size. Tailwind utility classes for mobile optimization avoid custom CSS bloat. Shimmer animation uses CSS gradients + Tailwind animate-pulse (hardware-accelerated). Touch interaction follows iOS Human Interface Guidelines (44px targets) and Material Design specs. Horizontal scroll with snap-scroll for filters provides native app-like UX without JavaScript scroll libraries.

Using Vercel Blob for file storage (serverless-native, auto CDN, no infrastructure management). Next.js 14 App Router API routes for backend. Client-side validation + server-side validation (defense in depth). Storing blob URL in Stripe metadata (max 500 chars per key, sufficient for URLs) rather than custom database to leverage existing Stripe webhook infrastructure. File sanitization with timestamp prefix + random suffix to prevent collisions. 10MB limit balances quality vs upload speed on mobile networks.

Using Next.js 14 App Router built-in Metadata API and MetadataRoute types (no external dependencies). Selected golden-retriever-renaissance.png as hero OG image (visually striking, representative). All metadata is statically generated at build time for optimal SEO. JSON-LD schemas follow Schema.org spec exactly to pass Google Rich Results Test. Sitemap uses standard priority/changeFrequency values for e-commerce sites.

Using sharp (pure JS, cross-platform) over sharp-cli for programmatic control and automation. Implementing automated data.ts updates via regex replacement to avoid manual merge errors. Next.js Image component with `fill` prop + aspect-ratio container recommended over fixed width/height for flexible grid layouts. WebP quality set to 82% (sweet spot for photos per Google best practices). Blur placeholders at 10×10px × 20% quality target ~1.5KB each for instant perceived performance without FOUC (Flash of Unstyled Content).

Phase 1 (technical foundation) is complete. Phase 2 focuses on revenue acceleration to reach $1M annual target. Current model ($9/portrait) requires 9,200 orders/month - unsustainable without premium tiers and automation. Strategy: implement tiered pricing (3x-10x revenue per customer), build conversion infrastructure (social proof, email capture), and automate fulfillment to scale to 300+ daily orders.

Using React 18 Suspense with Next.js 14 App Router for zero-config streaming SSR skeleton states. Tailwind utility-first approach for all responsive behavior (no custom media queries). Native CSS scroll-snap for performant touch scrolling without JavaScript. Following iOS Human Interface Guidelines for 44px minimum touch targets.
Creating design specs

Using Vercel Blob for storage (serverless-native, no infra setup). Client uploads to Next.js API route before Stripe checkout (not during payment). Blob URL stored in Stripe session metadata for artist reference post-purchase. File validation done both client-side (UX) and server-side (security). Public blob access for artist convenience.

Use Next.js 14 App Router native Metadata API (no react-helmet needed). Use route handlers (sitemap.ts/robots.ts) instead of static public/ files for maintainability. Use dangerouslySetInnerHTML for JSON-LD injection per React best practices. Set metadataBase in root layout to ensure absolute URLs. All zero-dependency implementation leveraging Next.js built-ins.

Using sharp (pure Node.js) instead of cwebp CLI for better cross-platform compatibility and TypeScript integration. Next.js Image component with responsive sizes tuned for 3-column desktop / 2-column tablet / 1-column mobile layout. Blur placeholders stored directly in data.ts for zero runtime overhead (static compilation). Quality set to 82% WebP (sweet spot for visual quality vs file size based on Google's research). Preserving original PNGs as backup for potential future reprocessing or higher-quality variants.

Transform Pawcasso Atelier into a high-performance, SEO-optimized e-commerce site by addressing critical image optimization bottlenecks, implementing modern SEO best practices, and enhancing the order flow with customer photo uploads. Focus on quick wins that improve Core Web Vitals and conversion rates.
